description: Tallest building in the world at completion, with observation decks on upper levels and fountain shows at its base
facts: At.mosphere restaurant occupied a high floor for years before other concepts rotated in
Tickets for sunset slots often sell out first
history: Emaar-led megaproject rebranded from Burj Dubai at opening; it anchors Sheikh Zayed Road's high-rise corridor
significance: The vertical exclamation point of Dubai's skyline marketing
description_ru: Самое высокое в мире здание после завершения строительства, с смотровыми палубами на верхних уровнях и фонтанными шоу у его основания
history_ru: Мегапроект под руководством Emaar, ранее известный как Burj Dubai, был переименован к открытию и является ориентиром высотного коридора Шейх Зайд Роуд
significance_ru: Вертикальный восклицательный знак маркетинга горизонтов Дубая
facts_ru: Ресторан At.mosphere занимал верхний этаж годами, прежде чем в нём сменились другие концепции
Билеты на вечерние сеансы часто раскупаются первыми

architecture_style: High-rise residential towers along artificial canal
description: Man-made marina lined with towers, cafés, and dhow dinner cruises. Evening light reflects in the water between walkways
facts: The Marina Walk is popular for jogging after dark when temperatures ease
Tram links connect to JBR beach without crossing busy roads
history: Excavated channel and reclaimed edges created a new waterfront property template west of the old creek trade city
significance: Densest cluster of residential supertalls in the emirate
description_ru: Искусственная марина, выстроенная вдоль башен, кафе и круизных ужинов на дхоу. Вечерний свет отражается в воде между пешеходными дорожками
history_ru: Раскопанный канал и рекультивированные края создали новую шаблонную недвижимость на набережной к западу от старого города торгового ручья
significance_ru: Самое плотное скопление жилых супервысоких зданий в эмирате
facts_ru: Мрина Ворк популярна для пробежек после наступления темноты, когда снижается температура
Трамвайные линии соединяются с пляжем JBR, не перекрещивая оживлённые дороги

architecture_style: Traditional dhow wharves and souk fabric

facts: Spice aromas and textile lanes sit a short walk from the abra docks
Al Fahidi's wind-tower neighbourhood lies on the Bur Dubai bank
history: Pearling and trade boats anchored here before oil revenues shifted the city's centre of gravity seaward
significance: The human-scale counterweight to Marina glass canyons
description_ru: Соленый вход, где встречаются текстильный и золотой рынки Абрас. Абрас до сих пор перевозят пассажиров за сбор в дирхамы
history_ru: Здесь причаливали жемчужные и торговые лодки до того, как нефтяные доходы сместили центр тяжести города в сторону моря
significance_ru: Масштаб человеческих размеров-противовес стеклянным каньонам Марины
facts_ru: Ароматы специй и лавки текстиля находятся в небольшой пешей доступности от причалов абра
Район парусных минаретов аль Фахиди находится на берегу Бур-Дубай

description: Luxury hotel on a reclaimed mound, visible along Jumeirah Beach. Interior atrium and restaurants are guest-reservation heavy
facts: Public beach viewpoints offer classic silhouette photos at blue hour
Helipad stunts in marketing made the crown world-famous
history: Tom Wright's sail metaphor became Dubai's first global architectural icon before the Burj Khalifa superseded height records
significance: Coastal landmark between old creek trade and Palm megaprojects
description_ru: Роскошный отель на восстановленном холме, с видом вдоль пляжа Джумейра. Интерьерный атриум и рестораны требуют предварительного бронирования от гостей
history_ru: Метафора парусности Тома Райта стала первой мировой архитектурной иконой Дубая до того, как Бурдж-Халифа превзошел рекорды высоты
significance_ru: Прибрежный объект между старой торговлей ручьем и мегапроектами "Пальма"
facts_ru: Виды с общедоступных пляжей позволяют сделать классические силуэтные фотографии в «синий час»
Показательные выступы с вертолетных площадок в рамках маркетинга сделали Королевство всемирно известным

facts: Modest dress is required for organised tours

history: Built as a gift from Sheikh Rashid; its programme of cultural outreach predates many newer museum initiatives
significance: Accessible introduction to mosque architecture away from the downtown towers
description_ru: Мечеть-камень продвигается для экскурсионных посещений «Открытые двери», на которых рассказывается об эмиратской культуре и молитвенной практике для посетителей

description_en: La Mer is a vibrant beachfront destination in Dubai, blending modernity with a laid-back atmosphere. Visitors can enjoy a variety of dining options, retail shops, and water sports, all set against the stunning backdrop of the Arabian Gulf. The area is designed for relaxation and entertainment, making it a popular spot for both locals and tourists alike.
description: La Mer is a vibrant beachfront destination in Dubai, blending modernity with a laid-back atmosphere. Visitors can enjoy a variety of dining options, retail shops, and water sports, all set against the stunning backdrop of the Arabian Gulf. The area is designed for relaxation and entertainment, making it a popular spot for both locals and tourists alike.
history_en: La Mer was developed as part of Dubai's ongoing efforts to enhance its coastal areas and promote tourism. Opened in 2017, this beachfront destination reflects the city's rapid growth and commitment to creating attractive leisure spaces.
history: La Mer was developed as part of Dubai's ongoing efforts to enhance its coastal areas and promote tourism. Opened in 2017, this beachfront destination reflects the city's rapid growth and commitment to creating attractive leisure spaces.
significance_en: Culturally, La Mer represents Dubai's shift towards a more relaxed lifestyle, offering a space for community engagement and leisure activities. It showcases the city's modern architectural style while also incorporating elements that reflect the local heritage. The area is a hub for social interaction, drawing diverse crowds that contribute to the multicultural fabric of Dubai.
significance: Culturally, La Mer represents Dubai's shift towards a more relaxed lifestyle, offering a space for community engagement and leisure activities. It showcases the city's modern architectural style while also incorporating elements that reflect the local heritage. The area is a hub for social interaction, drawing diverse crowds that contribute to the multicultural fabric of Dubai.
facts_en: La Mer spans over 2.5 kilometers along the coastline.
The area features over 130 shops, restaurants, and cafes.

description_en: City Walk Dubai is a vibrant urban destination that combines shopping, dining, and entertainment in a modern setting. Featuring a mix of high-end boutiques, casual eateries, and art installations, it offers a unique experience for both residents and visitors. The open-air design and contemporary architecture make it a popular spot for leisurely strolls and social gatherings.
description: City Walk Dubai is a vibrant urban destination that combines shopping, dining, and entertainment in a modern setting. Featuring a mix of high-end boutiques, casual eateries, and art installations, it offers a unique experience for both residents and visitors. The open-air design and contemporary architecture make it a popular spot for leisurely strolls and social gatherings.
history_en: City Walk Dubai was launched in 2015 as part of the larger development initiative by Meraas, aimed at creating a new urban lifestyle destination. The area has rapidly evolved into a hub for entertainment and retail, reflecting Dubai's growth as a cosmopolitan city.
history: City Walk Dubai was launched in 2015 as part of the larger development initiative by Meraas, aimed at creating a new urban lifestyle destination. The area has rapidly evolved into a hub for entertainment and retail, reflecting Dubai's growth as a cosmopolitan city.
significance_en: Culturally, City Walk Dubai represents the fusion of modernity and tradition, showcasing art and design while promoting community engagement through various events. It serves as a gathering place for locals and tourists alike, enhancing the social fabric of the city. The development aligns with Dubai's vision of becoming a global city, emphasizing innovation and lifestyle.
significance: Culturally, City Walk Dubai represents the fusion of modernity and tradition, showcasing art and design while promoting community engagement through various events. It serves as a gathering place for locals and tourists alike, enhancing the social fabric of the city. The development aligns with Dubai's vision of becoming a global city, emphasizing innovation and lifestyle.
facts_en: City Walk Dubai covers an area of 1.2 million square feet.
It features over 300 retail outlets and a wide array of dining options.
facts: City Walk Dubai covers an area of 1.2 million square feet.

description: Hatta (Arabic: حتا locally [ˈħɑttɐ]) is an inland exclave of the emirate of Dubai in the United Arab Emirates. Formerly Omani territory, its ownership was transferred to Dubai in or around 1850. Geography It lies to the south-east of Dubai's main territory and is about 134 km (83 mi) east of Dubai
facts: Check opening hours and ticketing on official sites before travel
Crowds peak on weekends and public holidays
description_ru: Хатта (араб. حتا, местное произношение [ˈħɑttɐ]) — внутренний эксклав эмирата Дубай, расположенный в Объединенных Арабских Эмиратах. Ранее это была оманская территория, и ее владение было передано Дубаю приблизительно в 1850 году. География Хатта расположена к юго-востоку от основной территории Дубая и находится примерно в 134 км (83 миль) к востоку от Дубая
description_en: Hatta (Arabic: حتا locally [ˈħɑttɐ]) is an inland exclave of the emirate of Dubai in the United Arab Emirates. Formerly Omani territory, its ownership was transferred to Dubai in or around 1850. Geography It lies to the south-east of Dubai's main territory and is about 134 km (83 mi) east of Dubai
facts_en: Check opening hours and ticketing on official sites before travel
Crowds peak on weekends and public holidays

description_en: The Gold Souk in Deira is a vibrant market renowned for its dazzling array of gold jewelry and precious gems. Visitors can explore narrow lanes lined with shops showcasing intricate designs and traditional craftsmanship. The souk is not only a shopping destination but also a cultural experience, reflecting Dubai's rich trading history.
description: The Gold Souk in Deira is a vibrant market renowned for its dazzling array of gold jewelry and precious gems. Visitors can explore narrow lanes lined with shops showcasing intricate designs and traditional craftsmanship. The souk is not only a shopping destination but also a cultural experience, reflecting Dubai's rich trading history.
history_en: The Gold Souk was established in the 1940s, coinciding with Dubai's emergence as a trading hub in the region. Over the decades, it has grown significantly, becoming a focal point for gold trading in the Middle East. The market has witnessed various developments, including modernization efforts while retaining its traditional charm.
history: The Gold Souk was established in the 1940s, coinciding with Dubai's emergence as a trading hub in the region. Over the decades, it has grown significantly, becoming a focal point for gold trading in the Middle East. The market has witnessed various developments, including modernization efforts while retaining its traditional charm.
significance_en: Culturally, the Gold Souk represents Dubai's historical role as a center for trade and commerce, contributing to the city's identity as a global city. It reflects the rich heritage of craftsmanship and the importance of gold in local traditions, including wedding ceremonies and celebrations. The souk is a testament to the enduring legacy of Dubai's mercantile past.
significance: Culturally, the Gold Souk represents Dubai's historical role as a center for trade and commerce, contributing to the city's identity as a global city. It reflects the rich heritage of craftsmanship and the importance of gold in local traditions, including wedding ceremonies and celebrations. The souk is a testament to the enduring legacy of Dubai's mercantile past.
facts_en: The Gold Souk features over 300 retailers, primarily specializing in gold jewelry.
It is estimated that the Gold Souk contains around 10 tons of gold on display at any given time.
facts: The Gold Souk features over 300 retailers, primarily specializing in gold jewelry.
It is estimated that the Gold Souk contains around 10 tons of gold on display at any given time.

description_en: Deira Old Souk, also known as the Spice Souk, is a vibrant market located in the heart of Dubai's historic district. Visitors can immerse themselves in the rich aromas of spices, herbs, and traditional goods, making it a sensory delight. The souk offers a glimpse into the city's trading past and remains a bustling hub for both locals and tourists alike.
description: Deira Old Souk, also known as the Spice Souk, is a vibrant market located in the heart of Dubai's historic district. Visitors can immerse themselves in the rich aromas of spices, herbs, and traditional goods, making it a sensory delight. The souk offers a glimpse into the city's trading past and remains a bustling hub for both locals and tourists alike.
history_en: The Deira Old Souk has its roots in Dubai's early trading days, dating back to the 19th century when it served as a key marketplace for merchants. It has evolved over the years, reflecting the city's growth from a small fishing village to a global trade center. The souk has witnessed significant changes, especially during the oil boom in the 1970s, which transformed Dubai's economy and infrastructure.
history: The Deira Old Souk has its roots in Dubai's early trading days, dating back to the 19th century when it served as a key marketplace for merchants. It has evolved over the years, reflecting the city's growth from a small fishing village to a global trade center. The souk has witnessed significant changes, especially during the oil boom in the 1970s, which transformed Dubai's economy and infrastructure.
significance_en: Culturally, Deira Old Souk is a vital part of Dubai's heritage, showcasing traditional trading practices and local craftsmanship. It plays an essential role in daily life, where residents shop for spices and other goods, thus preserving local traditions. The souk's atmosphere and architecture reflect the historical essence of the region, contributing to Dubai's identity as a melting pot of cultures.
significance: Culturally, Deira Old Souk is a vital part of Dubai's heritage, showcasing traditional trading practices and local craftsmanship. It plays an essential role in daily life, where residents shop for spices and other goods, thus preserving local traditions. The souk's atmosphere and architecture reflect the historical essence of the region, contributing to Dubai's identity as a melting pot of cultures.
facts_en: Deira Old Souk is known for its extensive range of spices, herbs, and traditional Arabian goods.
The souk is located near Dubai Creek, which has historically been a significant trade route.
facts: Deira Old Souk is known for its extensive range of spices, herbs, and traditional Arabian goods.
The souk is located near Dubai Creek, which has historically been a significant trade route.

description_en: Kite Beach is a vibrant stretch of coastline in Dubai, known for its stunning views of the iconic Burj Al Arab and its lively atmosphere. This popular destination attracts both locals and tourists with its soft sands, water sports, and a variety of cafes and restaurants. Ideal for families and fitness enthusiasts, Kite Beach offers ample space for beach activities, jogging, and relaxation under the sun.
description: Kite Beach is a vibrant stretch of coastline in Dubai, known for its stunning views of the iconic Burj Al Arab and its lively atmosphere. This popular destination attracts both locals and tourists with its soft sands, water sports, and a variety of cafes and restaurants. Ideal for families and fitness enthusiasts, Kite Beach offers ample space for beach activities, jogging, and relaxation under the sun.
history_en: Kite Beach has developed over the years as part of Dubai's transformation into a global city. While specific historical milestones for the beach itself may not be documented, it reflects the broader evolution of Dubai's coastal areas since the late 20th century, when the city began to invest heavily in tourism and recreation.

description_en: Dubai Hills Park is a sprawling green oasis nestled in the heart of Dubai, offering a serene escape from the bustling city. With its beautifully landscaped gardens, walking trails, and recreational facilities, it serves as a perfect spot for families and outdoor enthusiasts. The park is designed to promote a healthy lifestyle and community engagement, making it a popular destination for both residents and visitors.

It reflects the city's commitment to enhancing the quality of life for its residents through accessible green spaces, which are increasingly important in urban planning. The park also serves as a venue for community events, fostering social ties and cultural exchange among diverse populations.

description_en: Dubai Opera is a stunning architectural masterpiece located in the heart of Downtown Dubai. This multi-format venue hosts a diverse range of performances, including opera, ballet, theater, and concerts, making it a cultural hub for both residents and visitors. With its striking design inspired by the traditional dhow boats, the opera house offers a unique experience for art enthusiasts and casual visitors alike.
description: Dubai Opera is a stunning architectural masterpiece located in the heart of Downtown Dubai. This multi-format venue hosts a diverse range of performances, including opera, ballet, theater, and concerts, making it a cultural hub for both residents and visitors. With its striking design inspired by the traditional dhow boats, the opera house offers a unique experience for art enthusiasts and casual visitors alike.
history_en: Dubai Opera was inaugurated on August 31, 2016, marking a significant addition to the city's cultural landscape. Designed by the renowned architect Janus Rostock, the venue reflects Dubai's ambition to become a global arts destination. Since its opening, it has hosted numerous high-profile performances and events, contributing to the city's reputation as a center for arts and culture.

The venue is also part of the broader initiative to elevate Dubai's status as a global city, attracting tourists and fostering a vibrant arts scene.

description_en: Wild Wadi Waterpark is a premier water attraction located in Dubai, offering a variety of thrilling water rides and attractions for all ages. Set against the backdrop of the iconic Burj Al Arab, the park features wave pools, lazy rivers, and adrenaline-pumping slides, making it a perfect destination for families and adventure seekers alike. With its Arabian-themed design and lush landscaping, Wild Wadi provides a unique blend of fun and relaxation in the heart of the city.

Set against the backdrop of the iconic Burj Al Arab, the park features wave pools, lazy rivers, and adrenaline-pumping slides, making it a perfect destination for families and adventure seekers alike. With its Arabian-themed design and lush landscaping, Wild Wadi provides a unique blend of fun and relaxation in the heart of the city.
history_en: Wild Wadi Waterpark opened its doors in 1999 and was developed by the Jumeirah Group, which is known for its luxury hospitality offerings in Dubai. The park has since become a key attraction in the city, drawing millions of visitors each year.

description_en: Alserkal Avenue is a vibrant cultural hub located in the Al Quoz district of Dubai, known for its contemporary art galleries, creative spaces, and innovative events. This dynamic venue fosters a community of artists, designers, and cultural enthusiasts, making it a must-visit destination for anyone interested in the arts. With its eclectic mix of exhibitions, performances, and workshops, Alserkal Avenue is a testament to Dubai's growing reputation as a center for creativity and cultural exchange.
description: Alserkal Avenue is a vibrant cultural hub located in the Al Quoz district of Dubai, known for its contemporary art galleries, creative spaces, and innovative events. This dynamic venue fosters a community of artists, designers, and cultural enthusiasts, making it a must-visit destination for anyone interested in the arts. With its eclectic mix of exhibitions, performances, and workshops, Alserkal Avenue is a testament to Dubai's growing reputation as a center for creativity and cultural exchange.
history_en: Alserkal Avenue was established in 2007, transforming a series of industrial warehouses into a thriving arts district. The initiative was spearheaded by a group of local art enthusiasts and entrepreneurs who recognized the need for a dedicated space to promote contemporary art and culture in Dubai. Over the years, it has evolved into a key player in the regional art scene, hosting numerous exhibitions and events that attract both local and international audiences.

significance_en: Culturally, Alserkal Avenue plays a crucial role in Dubai's artistic landscape, showcasing a diverse range of contemporary art and design. It serves as a platform for emerging and established artists, contributing to the city's cultural dialogue and fostering a sense of community. The avenue hosts various events, including art fairs, film screenings, and workshops, which engage the public and promote cultural awareness. While it does not hold religious significance, its secular nature allows for a wide range of artistic expressions that reflect the multicultural fabric of Dubai.

description_en: The Grand Mosque in Dubai, also known as the Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, is a stunning architectural masterpiece that showcases the rich cultural heritage of the UAE. With its intricate design and majestic domes, it serves as a prominent symbol of Islamic faith and artistry. Visitors are welcome to explore its serene surroundings and admire the beautiful prayer halls and courtyards.
description: The Grand Mosque in Dubai, also known as the Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, is a stunning architectural masterpiece that showcases the rich cultural heritage of the UAE. With its intricate design and majestic domes, it serves as a prominent symbol of Islamic faith and artistry. Visitors are welcome to explore its serene surroundings and admire the beautiful prayer halls and courtyards.
history_en: The Grand Mosque was inaugurated in 1998 and has since become one of the largest mosques in the United Arab Emirates. It was named after Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, the founding father of the UAE, and has been a site for significant religious events and gatherings.
history: The Grand Mosque was inaugurated in 1998 and has since become one of the largest mosques in the United Arab Emirates. It was named after Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, the founding father of the UAE, and has been a site for significant religious events and gatherings.
significance_en: The Grand Mosque plays a vital role in the cultural and religious landscape of Dubai. It is not only a place of worship but also a center for community events and interfaith dialogue. The mosque's architecture reflects Islamic art and heritage, making it a key attraction for both locals and tourists, while also promoting the values of peace and tolerance.

facts_en: The mosque can accommodate over 40,000 worshippers.

description_en: Al Fahidi Historical District, also known as Al Bastakiya, is a captivating area in Dubai that showcases the city's rich heritage and traditional architecture. Visitors can explore narrow lanes lined with wind-tower buildings, art galleries, and museums that reflect the Emirati culture. This district offers a glimpse into Dubai's past, making it a must-visit for those interested in the city's history and traditions.
description: Al Fahidi Historical District, also known as Al Bastakiya, is a captivating area in Dubai that showcases the city's rich heritage and traditional architecture. Visitors can explore narrow lanes lined with wind-tower buildings, art galleries, and museums that reflect the Emirati culture. This district offers a glimpse into Dubai's past, making it a must-visit for those interested in the city's history and traditions.
history_en: Al Fahidi Historical District dates back to the late 19th century and was originally established by Persian merchants. The area served as a trading hub and residential quarter, reflecting the architectural style and lifestyle of the time. Significant restoration efforts began in the early 2000s to preserve its historical value and promote cultural tourism.
history: Al Fahidi Historical District dates back to the late 19th century and was originally established by Persian merchants. significance_en: Culturally, Al Fahidi Historical District is a vital part of Dubai's identity, representing the traditional lifestyle and heritage of the Emirati people. The district hosts various cultural events and art exhibitions, contributing to the local arts scene. Its preservation is crucial for understanding Dubai's evolution from a small fishing village to a global metropolis.

description_en: Hatta Heritage Village is a captivating glimpse into the traditional life of the UAE, showcasing the region's rich history and culture. Visitors can explore restored buildings, including a fort and several houses, that reflect the architectural styles of the past. The village offers a unique opportunity to experience local crafts, traditional customs, and the natural beauty of the Hatta region.

description_en: Qasr Al Hosn is a historic fort in the heart of Abu Dhabi, symbolizing the UAE's rich heritage and cultural identity. Originally built in the 18th century, it served as a watchtower and later became the residence of the ruling family. Today, it stands as a museum, showcasing the history and traditions of the Emirati people.
description: Qasr Al Hosn is a historic fort in the heart of Abu Dhabi, symbolizing the UAE's rich heritage and cultural identity. Originally built in the 18th century, it served as a watchtower and later became the residence of the ruling family. Today, it stands as a museum, showcasing the history and traditions of the Emirati people.
history_en: Qasr Al Hosn was constructed in 1761, making it the oldest stone building in Abu Dhabi. Initially built as a watchtower to protect the freshwater wells, it evolved into a royal palace and the center of governance for the emirate. The fort has undergone several renovations and restorations, particularly in the late 20th century, to preserve its historical significance.

The fort's transformation into a museum underscores its role in educating visitors about the region's past and fostering a sense of national pride.

description_en: Expo City Dubai is a vibrant hub that showcases innovation, sustainability, and culture, built on the legacy of Expo 2020. Visitors can explore pavilions that celebrate global achievements and experience interactive exhibits that highlight the future of technology and design. The city is a testament to Dubai's commitment to creating a sustainable urban environment and fostering international collaboration.
description: Expo City Dubai is a vibrant hub that showcases innovation, sustainability, and culture, built on the legacy of Expo 2020. Visitors can explore pavilions that celebrate global achievements and experience interactive exhibits that highlight the future of technology and design. The city is a testament to Dubai's commitment to creating a sustainable urban environment and fostering international collaboration.
history_en: Expo City Dubai was established as the site for Expo 2020, which was postponed to 2021 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The expo ran from October 1, 2021, to March 31, 2022, and marked the first World Expo held in the Middle East. Following the event, the area has been transformed into a permanent destination for culture, education, and innovation.
history: Expo City Dubai was established as the site for Expo 2020, which was postponed to 2021 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The expo ran from October 1, 2021, to March 31, 2022, and marked the first World Expo held in the Middle East. Following the event, the area has been transformed into a permanent destination for culture, education, and innovation.
significance_en: Expo City Dubai holds cultural significance as it embodies the spirit of global collaboration and innovation. The site features pavilions from various countries, showcasing their heritage and advancements, thus fostering a sense of community and shared knowledge. It serves as a platform for cultural exchange and highlights Dubai's role as a leader in sustainability and urban development.

Expo 2020 attracted over 24 million visitors from around the world.

description_en: The Jumeirah Mosque is one of Dubai's most iconic landmarks, known for its stunning architecture and welcoming atmosphere. Open to non-Muslim visitors, it offers guided tours that provide insights into Islamic culture and traditions. The mosque's intricate design and serene surroundings make it a popular spot for both tourists and locals alike.
description: The Jumeirah Mosque is one of Dubai's most iconic landmarks, known for its stunning architecture and welcoming atmosphere. Open to non-Muslim visitors, it offers guided tours that provide insights into Islamic culture and traditions. The mosque's intricate design and serene surroundings make it a popular spot for both tourists and locals alike.
history_en: Completed in 2000, the Jumeirah Mosque was built in the traditional Fatimid style, which is characterized by its large dome and twin minarets. It was constructed to promote cultural understanding and tolerance, serving as a key site for interfaith dialogue in the region.
history: Completed in 2000, the Jumeirah Mosque was built in the traditional Fatimid style, which is characterized by its large dome and twin minarets. It was constructed to promote cultural understanding and tolerance, serving as a key site for interfaith dialogue in the region.
significance_en: Culturally, the Jumeirah Mosque plays a vital role in fostering understanding between different faiths and cultures in Dubai. It stands as a symbol of the UAE's commitment to tolerance and coexistence. The mosque is also a significant site for daily prayers and community gatherings, reflecting the importance of Islam in the daily lives of many residents.

It is designed in the Fatimid architectural style, featuring a large central dome and two minarets.
